title: Beyond the Heap: Mastering Deterministic Memory with Project Panama
description: Stop fighting with DirectByteBuffers and JNI. Learn how Project Panama's Arena API provides a safe, deterministic way to manage native memory in high-performance Java systems.

title: The Hidden Latency of Lock-Free Rate Limiters: Lessons from Uber's Scale
description: Learn why simple AtomicLong rate limiters fail under high contention and how to design for mechanical sympathy. A deep look at cache line bouncing and the lazy refill pattern.

title: The Hidden Race: Solving Distributed Cache Inconsistency in Java Systems
description: Learn why the standard 'delete-on-write' cache strategy fails in high-traffic Java applications and how to implement versioned invalidation to prevent stale data.

title: Why Your Service Still Crashes Despite Having Timeouts: The Bulkhead Pattern
description: Learn why timeouts aren't enough to prevent cascading failures. We explore Netflix's isolation strategies to protect your thread pools from slow downstream dependencies.

title: Beyond the Table: Why Column Families are the Secret to Bigtable Performance
description: Stop treating NoSQL column families like relational columns. Learn how Bigtable's physical storage model dictates your system's latency and how to design schemas for massive scale.

title: Beyond Sticky Sessions: Mastering Causal Tokens for Read-Your-Own-Writes
description: Learn why 'Session Consistency' is more than just a configuration toggle. We explore the mechanics of causal tokens in Java to prevent stale reads in distributed systems.

title: Beyond Distributed Locks: How Apple’s FoundationDB Scales Global Consistency
description: Stop reaching for distributed locks. Learn how decoupling sequencing from storage allows systems to achieve strict serializability without the performance tax of traditional locking mechanisms.

title: Virtual Threads Are Not Enough: Why Structured Concurrency Changes How You Reason About Concurrent Code
description: Virtual threads solve throughput. Structured concurrency solves correctness. This article explains why the second half of Project Loom matters more for writing concurrent code you can actually reason about.
